title: Rare Evo and Dev Gov Day 2026: Cardano Title Sponsorship

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (expired), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with current VIP ticket retail value listed as $900." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, valued by the proposer at $660,000 using $0.24 per ADA, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open. What would change this vote to YES: Provide budget analysis; Provide feasibility assessment. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.63; confidence 64.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano title sponsorship for Rare Evo 2026 main stage/global livestream and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026 sponsorship.

Deliverables: Cardano title sponsorship of Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream, Cardano title sponsorship/support for Rare Dev Gov Day 2026 on July 28, 2026 at ARIA Las Vegas, Free General Admission access for the Cardano community, Complimentary booth space for selected Cardano ecosystem startup projects, Five Cardano-focused main stage panelist positions, Breakout room and workshop space, Travel and lodging support for 20 Cardano community members, 10 ARIA hotel room giveaways and 50 VIP conference pass giveaways, Tier-1 media coverage and livestream Cardano ads, Return of 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury
Deadline/expiry: Events scheduled for July 28-31, 2026 in Las Vegas; specific delivery deadline not otherwise stated in document
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, valued by the proposer at $660,000 using $0.24 per ADA, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed budget allocates ₳1,166,666.67 to Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026, ₳833,333.33 to Rare Evo 2026 title sponsorship, and ₳750,000 to Intersect, audit, fees, taxes, and a Dev Gov Day 2027 deposit.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with current VIP ticket retail value listed as $900.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says funding would substantially expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and industry outreach.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Rare Network claims it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The document reports Rare Evo 2025 metrics including 2,500 annual attendance, 80 exhibitor booths, 85 speakers, 100+ ecosystem partners, 20K+ livestream views, 4M social impressions, and 40+ Cardano projects represented.
missing: Independent evidence for: Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with current VIP ticket retail value listed as $900.

Independent evidence for: Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says funding would substantially expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and industry outreach.
Independent evidence for: Rare Network claims it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that sponsorship benefits may change if another sponsor purchases the mainstage or livestream package before approval, with Cardano receiving an equal impact or dollar value sponsorship instead.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, valued by the proposer at $660,000 using $0.24 per ADA, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with current VIP ticket retail value listed as $900." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
